task: Write a Python function kth_ordered(words, k) returning the k-th word, counting from 1, when the words are ordered by increasing length, and words of equal length are ordered reverse-alphabetically among themselves. Return None when k is out of range. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all the requirements, handling an edge case of an empty list.
trace:
** 
I approached this problem by first grouping the words based on their lengths using a dictionary. This allows for efficient sorting and selection of words according to the given criteria. After creating the grouped dictionary, I sorted each group reverse-alphabetically and then extended them into a single list named `sorted_words`. Finally, I returned the k-th word if it falls within the range of the list; otherwise, I returned None. This approach ensures that the function is both correct and concise, adhering to the criteria provided.
